description_long: This is a vintage full-size computer keyboard with a beige plastic chassis and an array of keys, including a standard alphanumeric section, function keys, a numeric keypad, and additional programmable or macro keys along the top. The keyboard features a row of indicator lights and specialized buttons, likely for system or media control, above the main key area. The casing is robust and slightly yellowed, suggesting age and frequent use, with visible mechanical key switches beneath some missing keycaps. On the rear, there are two large cylindrical feet or mounting brackets, possibly for attachment to a workstation or for adjustable tilt. The underside reveals molded plastic details, ventilation grilles, and a label area, with no visible cable or wireless receiver attached. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on functionality and durability, typical of professional or industrial keyboards from the late 20th century.
description: A vintage, full-size beige computer keyboard with a numeric keypad, function keys, and additional programmable buttons, showing signs of age and use. The keyboard has large rear mounting brackets and some missing keycaps, revealing mechanical switches.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the top and left side, highlighting the full key layout, missing keycaps, and the left cylindrical mounting bracket.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the entire key arrangement, including the function row, numeric keypad, and all programmable keys, with both mounting brackets visible.
description_view_2: Rear angled view focusing on the back and left side, showing the cylindrical mounting brackets and the absence of keys or cable connections.
description_view_3: Back view revealing the underside, molded plastic details, label area, and both mounting brackets, with no visible cable or receiver.
